scope.summary: Implement the 1:N segment sewing authoring pattern as a named SeamGroup type that maps one SeamEndpoint to an ordered list of N target SeamEndpoints with proportional gather distribution, validated as a composed SeamSpec chain
acceptance_criteria: A SeamGroup struct (or equivalent GarmentSpec field) accepts one from-endpoint and an ordered Vec<SeamEndpoint> of N target segments; each pair is decomposed into a SeamSpec with gather_ratio = from_length / (sum_of_to_lengths / N) at mesh generation
The solver emits correct constraint counts: N constraint groups covering the N sub-seams with proportional rest lengths
The SEAM_LENGTH_MATCH check confirms all N sub-seam pairs converge within tolerance at run end
The model-first API (author_garment / edit_panel) accepts 1:N sewing intent and the ModelManual documents the expected JSON shape
A round-trip test confirms 1:N seams survive GarmentSpec versioning and forward migration
scope.risk_if_missed: Waistband-to-skirt panels, cuff-to-sleeve joins, and collars attached to a neckline opening are canonical 1:N seams. Missing this forces operators to pre-split every neckline into N manually counted segments before sewing, which is error-prone and blocks parametric garment generation for any garment with a gathered attachment.

risks: Broad MT scope can hide multiple review targets.
Markdown MT lists may disagree with packet-declared active/next MT truth.
Receipt routing may target an old MT if contract identity is omitted.
minimum_controls: Exactly one primary proof target per MT unless the packet records why not.
MT identity must be stable and referenced by receipt correlation.
Generated projection drift must not wake Coder or Validator by itself.
